content: Hey, I'm Rowan Ramirez. I've been driving trucks for years, but I'm getting ready for a triathlon next month and I need to get my tech in order.
Hi Rowan, congratulations on taking on a triathlon! What kind of tech are you looking to upgrade or change for your training and racing?
I've been using a Garmin watch, but I'm not sure it's the best fit for me. I also love speedcubing in my free time, and I wish there was an app that could help me track my solves.
There are several great alternatives to Garmin watches. For speedcubing, you might enjoy an app like Cube Tracker or Speedcube Timer.
Those sound great, thanks! I'll look into them. I've also been trying to find a gardening app that can help me keep track of my beekeeping and gardening projects.

I'd recommend checking out apps like BeeKeeper or Garden Plan Pro. They can help you track your progress, note weather patterns, and even provide tips for improving your beekeeping and gardening skills.
Awesome, thanks! I'm really looking forward to trying those out. By the way, I'm training for the triathlon on the 20th of April, and I'm planning to do a 10K run, 40K bike ride, and 1K swim.

That sounds like a challenging but rewarding triathlon, Rowan! Make sure to stay hydrated and listen to your body during training. Have you considered using a nutrition planning app to help fuel your workouts?
That's a great idea, thanks! I've been eating a lot of Japanese cuisine to fuel my training, and I've found it really helps with my energy levels.
Japanese cuisine is a great choice for athletes, Rowan. The combination of complex carbs, protein, and healthy fats can provide sustained energy for your workouts.
I'm glad I made the switch. I've also been listening to podcasts during my long drives, and I'm looking for some new recommendations. Do you have any favorite podcasts?

I'm glad you enjoy podcasts, Rowan! I can suggest some popular ones like How I Built This or The Tim Ferriss Show. Let me know if you have any specific interests, and I can give you more tailored recommendations.
Thanks for the suggestions! I think I'll check those out.
